question: In the opening in Colossians Paul identifies himself (Col. 1:1)
options: As the servant of Jesus Christ
As a missionary to the church at Colossae
As an apostle of Jesus Christ
As a prophet of the Lord

question: Who does Paul identify as his brother in the opening of Colossians (Col. 1:1)?
What does Paul send from God our Father (Col. 1:2)
Paul addresses the letter to the brothers and sisters at Colossae as
In the greeting Paul sends grace and peace from _______ (Col. 1:2)
Who does Paul always thank for the Colossians (Col. 1:3)
What does Paul say the gospel is doing (Col. 1:6)
options: Spreading to the ends of the earth

question: From whom did the Colossians learn the gospel (Col. 1:7)
What had Epaphras told Paul (Col. 1:8)
options: About the Colossians’ love in the Spirit
About the Colossians’ faithfulness in all things

question: Paul desired they would live what kind of life (Col. 1:10)
options: A life worthy of the Lord
A life of wisdom and understanding
A life of joy and peace

question: Paul desired they would bear what type of fruit (Col. 1:10)
What did Paul desire they would grow in (Col. 1:10)?
To what end does Paul desire they would be strengthened with all power (Col. 1:11)?
options: That they may have hope and love
That they may have endurance and patience
That they may have humility and kindness
That they may enter the presence of God the Father

question: What has the Father qualified them to do (Col. 1:12)?
options: Share in the salvation of the saints
Share in the inheritance of his holy people

question: What is Christ described as the image of (Col. 1:15)
How does Paul relate the Son to the rest of creation (Col. 1:15)
Why were all things created (Col. 1:16)?
What is Christ’s relationship to the body (Col. 1:18)
What was God pleased to do in Christ (Col. 1:19)?
options: Have all his fullness dwell in Christ
Have all his glory manifest in Christ

question: What did God do through Christ (Col. 1:20)?
options: Created the sun, moon, and stars
Forgive the sins of the world

question: By what did God make peace (Col. 1:20)?
options: By the work of the Spirit

question: Paul tells the Colossians they were once considered God’s ______ (Col. 1:21)
What has God done by Christ’s physical body (Col. 1:22)
